name: Oportun
description_short: Oportun Arlington, Texas — Oportun offers personal and secured loans with in-person applications, cash payment at retail locations, and Spanish-language...
description_long:
Oportun is a consumer lender with physical branch locations across all 50 U.S. states and Washington, D.C., serving borrowers who may lack access to traditional banking products. The company is structured to accommodate applicants with limited credit histories, accepting government-issued IDs including consular IDs alongside standard driver's licenses and passports, and accepting proof of income through pay stubs or bank statements rather than requiring formal employment documentation.

The company offers two core loan products: unsecured personal loans and secured loans that use the borrower's vehicle as collateral. Secured loan applicants must provide a car title in their name, vehicle registration, and car photos at the time of application. Loan payments can be made in cash at thousands of retail locations — including grocery stores, corner stores, and pharmacies — through payment partners Dolex, Barri, CheckFreePay, MoneyGram (receive code 7991oportun), and PayNearMe. All payment partners accept cash; PayNearMe charges a $1.75 fee for cash payments and $1.49 for debit.

What distinguishes Oportun from many online-only lenders is its physical branch presence and cash-payment infrastructure. Borrowers who are unbanked or underbanked, prefer in-person service, or need to make payments in cash at neighborhood stores can do so without needing a checking account or debit card. Oportun also provides full Spanish-language service, making it accessible to a large segment of the Latino community. The company's mobile app includes a referral program offering a one-time $50 bonus.

Oportun is a legitimate option for borrowers who have been shut out by traditional lenders and need a structured installment loan with a predictable repayment schedule. However, the location page reviewed does not disclose APRs or loan amounts, so prospective borrowers should review the full loan agreement carefully before signing. Borrowers applying for secured loans should note that the vehicle serves as collateral and could be repossessed if payments are missed. The cash payment fee of $1.75 per transaction via PayNearMe adds cost over the life of the loan for borrowers who cannot pay by other means.

As a financial institution, this lender competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans. Many of these lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
address: Inside El Rancho, 1909 E Park Row Dr, Arlington, TX 76010

company_info.headquarters: Inside El Rancho, 1909 E Park Row Dr, Arlington, TX 76010

pros: In-person applications available at physical store locations across all 50 states and D.C.
Cash payments accepted at grocery stores, corner stores, and pharmacies through five payment partners
Accepts consular IDs — accessible to immigrant borrowers without U.S.-issued ID
Secured loan option available for those who may not qualify for unsecured credit
Full Spanish-language support (Español) throughout the application process
Mobile app with active referral bonus program ($50 per referral through 4/15)
Multiple payment channels: Dolex, Barri, CheckFreePay, MoneyGram, PayNearMe
cons: Secured loan uses car title as collateral — vehicle at risk if payments are missed
PayNearMe cash payments cost $1.75 per transaction; debit payments cost $1.49 — recurring fee over loan life
APR, loan amounts, and repayment terms are not disclosed on the location page reviewed
Proof of income and a bill with physical address (no P.O. Boxes) required — may exclude some applicants
No indication of soft-pull pre-qualification to check rates without a hard inquiry
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked borrowers who need to make loan payments in cash at retail locations
Immigrant borrowers with a consular ID or limited U.S. credit history
Spanish-speaking applicants who prefer full-service support in Spanish
Borrowers who prefer in-person applications over online-only lenders
diagnosis: Oportun is best for borrowers with thin or no credit histories — particularly immigrants and unbanked individuals — who need a structured installment loan with in-person service and cash payment options. The main caveat is that secured loan applicants put their vehicle at risk, and borrowers should confirm the APR before committing, as the location page does not surface rate information.
services: Secured personal loans (vehicle title as collateral)
In-person loan application at branch locations
Cash loan payments at grocery stores, pharmacies, and corner stores
